name: de Bont minimal (DBM) medium (PMC10308882)
organism_scope: Pseudomonas putida EM42 (and Escherichia coli MG1655 as control)
description: de Bont minimal (DBM) medium — a defined medium extracted from Turlin et al. (2023). Mapped to BiGG from the paper's stated composition.

provenance.citation: Turlin et al. Core and auxiliary functions of one-carbon metabolism in Pseudomonas putida exposed by a systems-level analysis of transcriptional and physiological responses. mSystems (2023). DOI: 10.1128/msystems.00004-23. [PMC10308882]
provenance.notes: Extracted from the primary paper (snippet: DBM medium contained 3.88 g L−1 K2HPO4, 1.63 g L−1 NaH2PO4, 2 g L−1 (NH4)2SO4, and 0.1 g L−1 MgCl2·6H2O, with the initial pH adjusted at 7.0. DBM medium was supplemented with a trace element solution ). Component names mapped to BiGG (see mapping_confidence); presence-based bounds unless the paper gave exchange bounds; concentration_mM where units allowed. Extraction confidence: high.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: DBM medium contained 3.88 g L−1 K2HPO4
provenance.base_medium: de Bont minimal medium (DBM), Hartmans/de Bont mineral medium

sources_chased.what: base medium cited as ref 34 (Hartmans, Smits, van der Werf, Volkering, de Bont 1989, Appl Environ Microbiol 55:2850-2855, PMC203180, doi:10.1128/aem.55.11.2850-2855.1989)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch of PMC203180 HTML and PDF returned only abstract/download-shell (methods not text-extractable); full DBM recipe including complete trace-element solution is already spelled out verbatim in the main text of PMC10308882, so no additional constituents were recoverable or needed

recuration.notes: The paper's main text gives the DBM recipe in full: phosphate buffer (K2HPO4 + NaH2PO4), (NH4)2SO4 as N/S source, MgCl2·6H2O, and the complete de Bont trace-element solution (EDTA chelator + Zn, Ca, Fe, Mo, Cu, Co, Mn salts). DBM is a defined medium requiring no vitamins/growth factors for P. putida; none are added. Standard carbon source is 20 mM glucose (methanol and formate are experiment-specific supplements, not part of the base medium). Cultivations are aerobic (P. putida is an obligate aerobe). All element exchanges in current_components are covered; concentrations and exact salt identi
